description: Capability coverage contract for the Codex-first NativeUI developer-agent surface. Tests keep rules, skills, tools, review gates, and mirrored docs synced when NativeUI gains new project features.

features.description: Every declared NativeUI target has an explicit create, edit, logic, run, test, and release disposition.
rules: Bare mobile uses the stable flagship pair, bare web uses authored HTML plus static, and bare desktop uses Rust; alternatives remain explicit.
Web, Rust, C#, desktop, and mobile requests resolve to registered target IDs and retain beta labels until their gates pass.
Present macOS SwiftUI as an Apple-native desktop alternative that requires a separately scoped/new exporter; never advertise iOS SwiftUI as desktop output.
Unknown capabilities are forbidden: declarations are implemented, compiled away, or gated for every target.

terms: fails when Android and iOS connector class sets diverge

features.description: project.json changes are guarded against stale cloud/editor writes.
rules: Check status before changing cloud-backed projects.
Push with expectedRevision and fail closed on stale writes.

features.description: API keys and database credentials are stored account-side, never in project/native/source artifacts.
rules: Register API/database library metadata in project.json.
Put secret values through account-side secret endpoints via stdin only.

terms: Store secrets only in account secret storage
Secrets are never written to project.json

features.description: Loose, non-HTML, interaction-free, or non-responsive inputs produce a tested NativeUI design guide before authoring.
rules: Invoke nativeui-design when the source is not production-ready HTML.
Capture delivery targets, responsive parent constraints, primary journey, motion, UX state, accessibility, and implementation guidance.

features.description: Backend/deployment work is gated by a tested architecture record and explicit approval.
rules: Audit existing repo/backend/deployment shape before choosing a stack.
Record exact client targets, web rendering and hosting, origins, auth/cache policy, and distribution.
Do not scaffold, deploy, or wire backend work until the architecture record is approved.

features.description: Clean/prod native export is the default; beta is reserved for parity instrumentation.
rules: Default exports should be runnable production-style native projects.
Use beta only when the parity harness is explicitly needed.

features.description: Repeaters preserve preview rows and require registered API/database data sources for live data.
rules: Provide sampleItems for native preview expansion.
Register live data sources as API/database library items before connector wiring.

features.description: Radial gradients are supported; conic/repeating gradients are still guarded as degraded import surface.
rules: Use supported linear-gradient/radial-gradient for native fidelity.
Warn on conic/repeating gradient usage.

features.description: Vanilla HTML, React, Vue, Angular, and Astro are explicit authored-source PWA lanes with static or SSR guidance and developer-owned seams.
rules: Default bare web requests to vanilla HTML plus static after offering all five lanes and render-mode tradeoffs.
Compile responsive layout and dynamic flow into lane-native source without iframes, shipped project models, or generic runtime interpreters.
Preserve app-actions, data-adapters, and custom-components while regenerating contracts and emitting .new candidates for changed implementations.

features.description: Rust desktop, iOS, Android, and web are registered first-class target lanes with a shared durable app_actions.rs seam and explicit beta gates.
rules: Resolve Rust requests to rust-desktop, rust-ios, rust-android, or rust-web and never silently omit a selected host.
Rust uses the asynchronous NuiActionResult contract through app_actions.rs while legacy synchronous hooks remain compatible.
